name: JClaw
//: Read by VS Code Dev Containers, GitHub Codespaces, Cursor, JetBrains Gateway, and any other devcontainer-spec-compliant IDE. Build from the local Dockerfile so the image carries a known-good toolchain (JDK 25, Node 24, corepack, Play 1.13.x, Python 3, tesseract-ocr) — no reliance on a generic base image plus features that may drift in version. The Dockerfile installs every prereq jclaw.sh setup's check_prereqs validates, so postCreateCommand never fails on a missing dep at this layer.
//build: Each developer's IDE builds this Dockerfile locally on first 'Reopen in Container' (~5-10 min, then cached). To switch to pulling a pre-built image instead — recommended once there are 3+ active contributors or Codespaces is in use — first run a Jenkins build with PUBLISH_DEVCONTAINER=true (publishes ghcr.io/tsukhani/jclaw-devcontainer:latest), then replace the build block below with: "image": "ghcr.io/tsukhani/jclaw-devcontainer:latest" — that's the only change needed here.
//context: Build context is repo root, not .devcontainer/, so the Dockerfile can COPY build.gradle.kts (whose `frameworkVersion.set(...)` line is parsed at image build time to determine which tsukhani/play1 release zip to download — single source of truth, no separate version pin file in 1.13.x).
//postCreateCommand: Runs the same setup script a human would invoke after a fresh clone, AFTER the image build finishes and the workspace is bind-mounted. Wires git hooks, runs corepack install + pnpm install, validates prereqs (which all pass thanks to the Dockerfile), and adds the github remote. Setup is idempotent, so re-running on container rebuild is harmless. The trailing `./gradlew playClasspath` + Playwright chromium install populate the JVM-side artifacts the Dockerfile couldn't pre-fetch — `playClasspath` triggers full Gradle dependency resolution (populating ~/.gradle/caches/) as a side effect, and we then grep its output for the Playwright + driver jar paths and feed those to the Playwright CLI's chromium installer. One-time cost on first container open; subsequent reopens find ~/.gradle/caches/ + ~/.cache/ms-playwright/ already populated and skip both steps fast (Gradle no-ops on a fully-resolved tree; Playwright CLI no-ops when chromium is already at the expected version). PF-90: `play deps` is gone, Gradle handles dependencies natively, no more lib/ directory.
postCreateCommand: ./jclaw.sh setup && PWCP=$(./gradlew -q playClasspath | grep -E 'playwright-[0-9.]+\.jar|driver-[0-9.]+\.jar' | tr '\n' ':') && [ -n "$PWCP" ] && java -cp "$PWCP" com.microsoft.playwright.CLI install chromium
//forwardPorts: Backend on 9000, Nuxt dev server on 3000 — match the defaults in jclaw.sh's BACKEND_PORT / FRONTEND_PORT.
//customizations: VS Code / Cursor extensions that match the project's stack. Vue + TypeScript for the frontend; Java extension pack for the backend; ESLint + Stylelint for the lint-staged pre-commit hook.
